etymology.description: semantic 氵(氵) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 工(工) (gōng) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The water radical 氵(氵) (shuǐ, "water") indicates the association with water, specifically a river. The 工(工) (gōng) provides the pronunciation, connecting the sound with the natural context of water courses.

examples.chinese: 这条江流经许多城镇(這條江流經許多城鎮)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo jiāng liújīng xǔduō chéngzhèn.
examples.english: This river flows through many towns.

examples.chinese: 江水捧起一片叶子(江水捧起一片葉子)，飘向远方(飄向遠方)。
examples.pinyin: Jiāngshuǐ pěng qǐ yī piàn yèzi, piāo xiàng yuǎnfāng.
examples.english: The river water lifted a leaf, floating it into the distance.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢在江边散步(我喜歡在江邊散步)，欣赏美景(欣賞美景)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài jiāng biān sànbù, xīnshǎng měijǐng.
examples.english: I like to walk along the river, enjoying the beautiful scenery.
